entry.title: Re: Determine if there is data in past 24 months of Actuals
entry.content:
Hi @raf_lim 

we had a similar request from business. Our approach to solve it was to first, make a new key figure. Within this key figure we manipulated the actuals, to either use the corresponding actuals in the past. If there were no actuals (isnull), we instead filled the key figure with a 0.

Next, we used an XYZ-segmentation. In the segmentation we used the manipulated actuals qty key figure mentioned above, added the requested horizon (in your case 24 months) with a calculation strategy "aggregate of periods", aggregation method "sum" and segmentation method "(1) by Threshold". By doing so, you can create a threshold in "smaller than or equal to" 0 which gives you a segment with no actuals, while evereything bigger than 0 has actuals.

Using a segmetation allows you to easily fill an attribute, that the end user can apply to filter in the excel add in or planner workspace.

If you have further questions (e.g. implementation screenshots), just reach out to me. I'm happy to help.

Best regards,
Matthias

entry.title: NewSalesJournalEntryVoucher Customisation --- Adding Standard Field Not Working
entry.content:
Hi,

I've followed https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blogs-by-sap/how-to-guide-for-journal-entry-voucher-excel-customization/ba-p/13291000 a few times before for extension fields, which has worked fine. This time I've been asked to try it with a standard field --- EmployeeID, at the AccountingEntry.Item level. I've added the below line to the XML map as shown in the guide:

And the actual Excel file lets me add the column, and doesn't complain when the information is saved; it creates a sales journal entry voucher. However the Employee ID is empty:

I also tried with data type xs:string instead of tns:IDType --- same result. I don't think it's a question of data type compatibility (the maximum length of IDType is given as 60 characters), as the External Reference at header level has a maximum length of 35 characters, but I'm happy to be proved wrong. Has anyone any ideas?

Thanks,

Lewis

  • Requirements Traceability

Solution Process Traceability

entry.title: Re: How to update individual customer information in the SDK
entry.content:
Hi,

I don't know if it's still relevant, but it used to be that sometimes trying to write information using those shortcut variables didn't work so you had to specify the full path. What I suggest is:

updateCustomer.AddressInformation.Address.DefaultEMail.URI.content = <new email>;

That might work for you.

Lewis

entry.title: Re: Search String for EBS leading zero
entry.content: Dear Sir,

entry.title: Re: debug doesn't work
entry.content: By default a patch solution is disabled --- you have to enable it (which disables the original solution). You'll be able to check that in the Implementation Manager window. Alternatively, if you follow the code in the debugger then it'll tell you which solution ID the script is running in --- it'll most likely show the original solution ID, not the one of your patch.

JDBC on SAP CPI:

entry.title: Re: Is EKET-ETENR = EKEH-ETENR ?
entry.content:
Please look at:

2805077 - EKEH-ETENR starts from 0001 for each release

Main messages:

  • EKEH-ETENR starts from 0001, when user creates a new release for a scheduling agreement.
  • EKEH-ETENR doesn't show the value of EKET-ETENR in the releases (there is no link between EKEH and EKET).

Link:

https://me.sap.com/notes/2805077/E

Kind regards Martin

entry.title: SAP BTP, Kyma runtime: APIRule migration - ORY Oathkeeper based OAuth2 handlers
entry.content:
Hello everyone! My first blogpost about migration to the newer version of APIRule explained the migration process for the noAuth and jwt handlers. In this post, I focus on the remaining ORY Oathkeeper handlers.

Overview

The latest version, 2.6.0, of API Gateway introduces the extAuth handler, in addition to the existing noAuth and jwt handlers. The extAuth handler allows for migration from ORY Oathkeeper-based OAuth2 handlers. Keep in mind that any other changes, beyond those explained in my blog posts, are not covered by high-availability migration and may cause downtime. I highly recommend reading the CRD documentation and following the API Gateway tutorials to learn more about extAuth configuration options.

In this blog post, I focus on the migration from oauth2_introspection handler as they are the most popular ORY Oathkeeper-based handlers. The tutorial assumes that you have an SAP BTP, Kyma runtime cluster with Istio and API Gateway modules added, and an HTTPBin Service in the Istio service mesh. The HTTPBin Service is exposed using an APIRule of type oauth2_introspection and is accessible with a token obtained from Cloud Identity Services.

Migrate APIRule v1beta1 of type oauth2_introspection to APIRule v2alpha1 of type extAuth

See the following example of APIRule v1beta1 that uses the oauth2_introspection handler:

 

apiVersion: gateway.kyma-project.io/v1beta1
kind: APIRule
metadata:
  name: httpbin
  namespace: test
spec:
  gateway: test/httpbin-gateway
  host: httpbin.example.com
  service:
    name: httpbin
    namespace: test
    port: 8000
  rules:
    - path: /headers
      methods: ["GET"]
      accessStrategies:
        - handler: oauth2_introspection
          config:
            required_scope: ["read"]
            introspection_url: "https://{IAS_TENANT}.accounts.ondemand.com/oauth2/introspect"
            introspection_request_headers:
              Authorization: "Basic $ENCODED_CREDENTIALS"

 

To retrieve the headers endpoint, you can use a token containing the "read" scope issued by your Cloud Identity Services tenant. The token is additionally checked at the introspection endpoint, which is secured by additional credentials. All Oathkeeper-based handlers must be migrated to the extAuth handler. This is the most complicated scenario to perform as it requires additional steps. First, you must deploy a service that will act as an external authorizer for Istio. In this blogpost, I use oauth2-proxy with the following configuration:

 

cat <<EOF > values.yaml
config:
  clientID: $CLIENT_ID
  clientSecret: $CLIENT_SECRET
  cookieName: ""
  cookieSecret: $COOKIE_SECRET
 
extraArgs:
  cookie-domain: "httpbin.example.com"
  oidc-issuer-url: https://{IAS_TENANT}.accounts.ondemand.com
  provider: oidc
  scope: "read"
  ...
EOF

 

This is just a subset of configuration. Refer to oauth2-proxy documentation to view all the options. Once you have the configuration prepared, install oauth2-proxy using the following commands:

 

kubectl create namespace oauth2-proxy
helm repo add oauth2-proxy https://oauth2-proxy.github.io/manifests
helm upgrade --install oauth2-proxy oauth2-proxy/oauth2-proxy -f values.yaml -n oauth2-proxy

 

Next, you must register oauth2-proxy as external authorizer in Istio. To do this, patch the Istio CR:

 

kubectl patch istio -n kyma-system default --type merge --patch '{"spec":{"config":{"authorizers":[{"name":"oauth2-proxy","port":80,"service":"oauth2-proxy.oauth2-proxy.svc.cluster.local","headers":{"inCheck":{"include":["x-forwarded-for", "cookie", "authorization"]}}}]}}}'

 

You're all set and ready to perform the APIRule migration. Apply the following APIRule:

 

apiVersion: gateway.kyma-project.io/v2alpha1
kind: APIRule
metadata:
  name: httpbin
  namespace: test
spec:
  hosts:
    - httpbin.kyma.example.com
  service:
    name: httpbin
    namespace: test
    port: 8000
  gateway: test/httpbin-gateway
  rules:
    - extAuth:
        authorizers:
          - oauth2-proxy
      methods:
        - GET
      path: /headers

 

The above example of APIRule delegates checking a token to previously configured oauth2-proxy. Active tokens will continue to work during migration, and the migration process will not disrupt any exposed or secured workloads.

Next steps

API Gateway 2.7.0 introduces a new short host feature, which completes the functional scope of APIRule v2alpha1. It's planned to be rolled out to the regular channel on October 28, 2024. Until March 31, 2025, you'll have the opportunity to test the functionalities of APIRule v2alpha1, the API, and the migration. It is expected that APIRule v2alpha1 will be promoted to the stable v2 version without any significant changes. Up until May 12, 2025 you will be required to migrate all your APIRules to version v2. After that time, APIRule v1beta1 will be removed from all clusters. Finally, on June 16, 2025, APIRule v2alpha1 will also be removed and the API Gateway module will only serve version v2. 

Summary

This is the last blogpost covering the APIRule migration topics. I explained the necessary steps using the example of oauth2_introspection handler. Additionally, I encourage you to refer to our documentation and tutorials for further information on the changes. If you have any concerns or feedback, leave us a question, leave us a question.

entry.title: Re: Configuration for manual Absence Days in IT2001
entry.content:
It seems like you are having SAP setup a bit in a garbage way if I may be honest. The schedule of the employee (infotype 7) decides when he has to work.

This is just so that the employee can request a holiday from Monday to Sunday and doesn't have to worry to not include weekends or for example wednesdays if he/she doesn't work on Wednesdays. Your request is not possible and like mentioned before something that shouldn't be done.

entry.title: Feature Request: Allow formatting for DURATION functions in SIGNAL
entry.content:
Hi,

currently there is a default formatting applied for the return of DURATION functions in SIGNAL. We would like to request an additional parameter that allows to format the output.

To give an example where this is necessary: When e.g. summing up weekdays, there are scenarios where we also want the result to be displayed in days, even when the sum is greater than 7. Currently we get a result of e.g. 1 week, which suggests a different duration than 7 weekdays. 

Thank you,

Anna

entry.title: Error XU058 - During goods movement
entry.content:
Hi,

The unit of measure for material is TO and decimal places allowed for TO is 3. The alternate UOM used is M which as decimal places 2.

We have activated batch level UOM conversion. When we are trying to post any goods movement with UOM as TO system is giving error message as XU058 - Error during conversion to alternative unit of meas. TO: Correct qty 1.001 TO

If I change the decimal place for M to 3 decimal then this error is not coming. 

Could anyone confirm if it is OK to increase decimal for UOM in CUNI in live system or not?

 

Thanks

Subhash

entry.title: Recap: SAP Stammtisch am 30.09.2024 im Fraunhofer IFF
entry.content:
Rückblick auf den letzten SAP Stammtisch Magdeburg beim Fraunhofer IFF

Am vergangenen Stammtisch drehte sich alles um das Thema "Datenräume", welchem wir uns im Technikum des Fraunhofer IFF näherten. Das Event war hybrid und ermöglichte sogar eine Teilnahme aus dem Urlaub in Kroatien! 🌍😄

♊Vortrag 1: Tobias Kutzler vom Fraunhofer IFF erläuterte, wie Datenräume und digitale Zwillinge Unternehmen helfen, Daten effizienter auszutauschen. Er betonte die Bedeutung von Verwaltungsschalen, sozusagen einem standartisierten digitalen Zwilling, um die Integration zu vereinfachen und Informationen zu vereinheitlichen.

☁️Vortrag 2: Jens Schwetje von BA Business Advice GmbH führte uns durch die Transformation von monolithischen Systemen zu hybriden Cloud-Landschaften. Er erklärte, warum hybride Multi-Cloud-Lösungen unverzichtbar sind und hob die Wichtigkeit eines zentralen Datenmanagements als „Single Source of Truth“ hervor.

🧐 Vortrag 3: Stefan Voigt präsentierte spannende Projekte des Mittelstand-Digital Zentrum Magdeburg. Besonders interessant war das Beispiel eines Escape-Rooms in der Elbfabrik des Fraunhofer IFF – ein Hands-on-Erlebnis für Technologieenthusiasten!

Vielen Dank an alle Referenten und Teilnehmer:innen für diesen inspirierenden Austausch!

Notieren Sie sich den nächsten SAP Stammtisch am 25. November, der voraussichtlich wie gewohnt bei uns im SAP University Competence Center (UCC) Magdeburg am Uniplatz 12 stattfinden wird. Für Januar planen wir etwas Besonderes – weitere Informationen folgen!

____________

Review of the Last SAP Meet-Up at Fraunhofer IFF in Magdeburg! 🚀

At the recent meet-up, everything revolved around the topic of "Data Spaces," which we explored at the Fraunhofer IFF's workshop. The event was hybrid, allowing participation even from a vacation spot in Croatia! 🌍😄

♊Presentation 1: Tobias Kutzler from Fraunhofer IFF explained how data spaces and digital twins help companies to exchange data more efficiently. He emphasized the importance of administrative shells, essentially a standardized digital twin, to simplify integration and unify information.

☁️Presentation 2: Jens Schwetje from BA Business Advice GmbH guided us through the transformation from monolithic systems to hybrid cloud landscapes. He explained why hybrid multi-cloud solutions are indispensable and highlighted the significance of central data management as a “Single Source of Truth.”

🧐 Presentation 3: Stefan Voigt showcased exciting projects from the Mittelstand-Digital Zentrum Magdeburg. Particularly interesting was the example of an escape room in the Elbfabrik at Fraunhofer IFF – a hands-on experience for technology enthusiasts!

A big thank you to all speakers and participants for this inspiring exchange!

Mark your calendars for the next SAP Meet-up on November 25, which will likely take place at our usual spot, the SAP University Competence Center (UCC) Magdeburg at Uniplatz 12. We are planning something special for January – stay tuned for more details!

entry.title: BAPI_MATERIAL_SAVEDATA error requiried field
entry.content:
Hello, there.

I'm using the BAPI_MATERIAL_SAVEDATA to change some materials. (Just want to change a unique field MARC-MFRGR).

The problem is the BAPI returns an error:

"The field MARC-MTVFP/BAPI_MARC-AVAILCHECK is defined as a required field; it does not contain an entry"

Strange thinks:

- The material is modified correctly despite the error.

- The material doesn't have created the view where this field (MARC-MTVFP) is located. 

If I go to MM02 and change the material it's ok, no error is showed. If I create the view where the field is located then I see the error in MM02. 

Why the bapi is returning an error message asking for a required field in a view that doesnt exist in the material?

The code I'm using is prety simple: 

 


  ls_headdata-material = p_ls_datos-matnr.

  ls_plantdata-plant      = p_ls_datos-werks.
  ls_plantdata-matfrgtgrp = p_ls_datos-mfrgr.

  ls_plantdatax-plant      = p_ls_datos-werks.
  ls_plantdatax-matfrgtgrp = abap_true.

  CALL FUNCTION 'BAPI_MATERIAL_SAVEDATA'
    EXPORTING
      headdata       = ls_headdata
      plantdata      = ls_plantdata
      plantdatax     = ls_plantdatax
    IMPORTING
      return         = ls_return
    TABLES
      returnmessages = lt_returnmessages.

Thanks in advance.

PS: We are using SAP ECC 6.0

entry.title: Re: Mass Maintenance Order Printing Issue
entry.content:
Hi 

Your answers as below 

 in SAP Hana, can we do bulk print for PM order through IW38 ?

You should be able to use the List edit transaction iw38 to select and print Multiple Orders simultaneosuly. Once you have selected the orders in the Output screen, use the menu path ORDER > PRINT ORDER.

Print view from list not allowed. Papers will be created

Message no. IW257

Diagnosis

You requested the print view.

This view is not available from the list editing function.

System Response

The system cannot perform the function.

Procedure

You can only call up the print view from the individual processing function.

 

In this case, you need to select individual form and you can see print view it still gives error then there is a problem in program. You need to check with abap consultant 

 

Regards

Rashid Khan

entry.title: Use SAP Datasphere as a source for the SAP BW bridge
entry.content:
SAP Datasphere offers a rich set of connectors to access data in SAP and non-SAP systems and to physically load it into SAP Datasphere or virtually access the data in its original location. However, some scenarios might require it to consume data located in SAP Datasphere. One of the components where such a scenario might exist is the SP BW Bridge.

Exposing data from SAP Datasphere is quite straight forward.

​With CDS External Entities there is the possibility to create a RemoteSource with Remotetables. (Communication arrangement, Communication Scenario, Logical External Schema).

Let's take a regular View  in SAP Datasphere which must be exposed for consumption:

​The Datasphere OpenSQL User can be used to create this type of new connection. In addition a Communication Arrangement / -Scenario must be defined.

​All Datasphere Entities, which are enabled for external consumption, can then be used as CDS external entity in the BW Bridge.

​CDS external entities can be used in ABAP code to read the data from Datasphere. For further automation and ETL a custom Process Chain type can be created to include it as a step in a SAP BW/4HANA Process Chain and write the data via the BW Data API to a DataStore or InfoObject:

entry.title: Issue with Enabling/Disabling DateTimePicker Fields Based on Conditions in SAP UI5 Fragment
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I’m new to UI5 and currently working on a UI5 application that involves fetching employee details such as employee ID, name, last login time, and last logout time. In my application, I have a table where each row shows these details. An edit icon appears when either the last login or last logout time is available. If both fields are empty, the edit icon will not appear. The edit icon triggers the following function on press:

onEditPress : function(oEvent){
    var oSelectedValue = oEvent.getSource().getBindingContext("MODEL").getObject();
    var oSelectedRowModel = new sap.ui.model.json.JSONModel(oSelectedValue);
    this.getView().setModel(oSelectedRowModel, "selectedRow");

    if(!this._editLoginLogOutDialog){
        this._editLoginLogOutDialog = sap.ui.xmlfragment("fragment.EditLoginLogout", this);
    }
    this.getView().addDependent(this._editLoginLogOutDialog);
    this._editLoginLogOutDialog.open();
}

This opens a fragment dialog to edit the last login and logout times. The values are passed correctly, and I’m using formatters to enable or disable the fields based on the following logic:

  • If both last login and last logout have values, both fields should be editable.
  • If one field has a value, that field should remain editable, and the other should be disabled.

This is the fragment code

<DateTimePicker
placeholder="YYYY/MM/DD HH:MM:SS"
width="450px" class="sapUiLargeMarginBottom"
displayFormat="yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss"
value="{selectedRow>/LastLogin}"
valueFormat="yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss"
enabled="{parts: ['selectedRow>/LastLogout', 'selectedRow>/LastLogin'], formatter: '.isLoginEnabled'}" />


<DateTimePicker
placeholder="YYYY/MM/DD HH:MM:SS"
width="450px" class="sapUiLargeMarginBottom"
displayFormat="yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss"
value="{selectedRow>/LastLogout}"
valueFormat="yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss"
enabled="{parts: ['selectedRow>/LastLogin', 'selectedRow>/LastLogout'], formatter: '.isLogoutEnabled'}" />


Here are the formatters:

isLoginEnabled: function(sLastLogout, sLastLogin) {
    if ((sLastLogin && sLastLogin !== "") && (sLastLogout && sLastLogout !== "")) {
        return true;
    }
    if (sLastLogout && sLastLogout !== "") {
        return false;
    }
    return true;
},

isLogoutEnabled: function(sLastLogin, sLastLogout) {
    if ((sLastLogin && sLastLogin !== "") && (sLastLogout && sLastLogout !== "")) {
        return true;
    }
    if (sLastLogin && sLastLogin !== "") {
        return false;
    }
    return true;
},

The issue:
The code working fine, but when the user removes the value from one field (either last login or last logout), the other field becomes editable, which should not happen. I need to prevent this behavior.

Alternate approach I'm considering:
To avoid this issue, I’m thinking of disabling manual input so that users can only select values using the DateTimePicker popup and not manually type into the fields. I’ve tried various methods, but I haven’t been able to disable manual input while still allowing value selection through the popup.

Has anyone faced a similar issue or knows how to implement this behavior? Any advice would be greatly appreciated.

Thanks in advance!

SAPUI5 

SAP Young Thinkers

entry.title: Re: Still to be delivered value showing wrong for Service...
entry.content:
What you are seeing is correct - as Services differ from Materials, there is no physical "delivery" of stock, as there is none to stockpile / store;

(an example to think of is say, you have a person providing Window Cleaning services, you cannot stockpile or reserve the actual cleanings, this is a unstockable item).

The 'to be Delivered' section in Service POs are not accessed / used as a result.

Services are booked in via another transaction (ML81N) so are visible there in the entries for it (use report MSIS instead of the Material / Stock reports).

Hope this helps!

entry.title: Extract KNVI Customer Master Tax Indicator Data in CSV File Using ABAP Program
entry.content:
ABAP Program -  Z_KNVI_CUSTOMER

  • Program Type – ABAP 
  • ABAP Program Name – Z_KNVI_CUSTOMER
  • Tcode - Z_KNVI_CUST 
  • Output File Location - “C:\tmp” 
  • Output File Name- KNVI.CSV 
  • Output File Type – CSV (Column with Semicolon separator (;) 
  • SAP Table Name – KNVI, KNB1 
  • List of fields to be extracted -  KUNNR , TATYP 

-------ABAP PROGRAM STARTS FROM HERE---------------------

REPORT Z_KNVI_CUSTOMER.

TYPE-POOLS: VRM.

DATA: NAME TYPE VRM_ID,
LIST TYPE VRM_VALUES,
VALUE LIKE LINE OF LIST.


PARAMETERS: ENTITY(10) AS LISTBOX VISIBLE LENGTH 10.
PARAMETERS : OPATH TYPE STRING DEFAULT '\\SERVERNAME\tmp\KNVI.csv'.

AT SELECTION-SCREEN OUTPUT.

NAME = 'ENTITY'.

VALUE-KEY = 'UK01'.
VALUE-TEXT = 'UK01'.
APPEND VALUE TO LIST.

VALUE-KEY = 'UK75'.
VALUE-TEXT = 'UK75'.
APPEND VALUE TO LIST.

VALUE-KEY = 'UK21'.
VALUE-TEXT = 'UK21'.
APPEND VALUE TO LIST.

VALUE-KEY = 'UK31'.
VALUE-TEXT = 'UK31'.
APPEND VALUE TO LIST.

VALUE-KEY = 'UK41'.
VALUE-TEXT = 'UK41'.
APPEND VALUE TO LIST.

VALUE-KEY = 'UK02'.
VALUE-TEXT = 'UK02'.
APPEND VALUE TO LIST.

VALUE-KEY = 'UK73'.
VALUE-TEXT = 'UK73'.
APPEND VALUE TO LIST.

VALUE-KEY = 'UK04'.
VALUE-TEXT = 'UK04'.
APPEND VALUE TO LIST.

VALUE-KEY = 'UK61'.
VALUE-TEXT = 'UK61'.
APPEND VALUE TO LIST.

CALL FUNCTION 'VRM_SET_VALUES' EXPORTING ID = NAME VALUES = LIST.

START-OF-SELECTION.

type-pools:TRUXS.
data: begin of itab occurs 0,
KUNNR like KNVI-KUNNR,
VKBUR like KNVI-TATYP,
BUKRS like knb1-BUKRS,
end of itab.
data: itab1 type TRUXS_T_TEXT_DATA.
SELECT KNVI~KUNNR
KNVI~TATYP
KNB1~BUKRS
FROM KNVI INNER JOIN KNB1 ON KNVI~KUNNR = KNB1~KUNNR
into table itab
WHERE KNB1~BUKRS = ENTITY.

CALL FUNCTION 'SAP_CONVERT_TO_CSV_FORMAT'
EXPORTING
I_FIELD_SEPERATOR = ','
TABLES
I_TAB_SAP_DATA = itab
CHANGING
I_TAB_CONVERTED_DATA = itab1
EXCEPTIONS
CONVERSION_FAILED = 1
OTHERS = 2
.
IF SY-SUBRC <> 0.
MESSAGE ID SY-MSGID TYPE SY-MSGTY NUMBER SY-MSGNO
WITH SY-MSGV1 SY-MSGV2 SY-MSGV3 SY-MSGV4.
ENDIF.

CALL FUNCTION 'GUI_DOWNLOAD'
EXPORTING
filename = OPATH
TABLES
data_tab = itab1
EXCEPTIONS
OTHERS = 1.

entry.title: Re: EML READ on non-key field
entry.content:
That may not be possible.

I had a situation where a validation would require a similar logic, but it turned out that I needed to improve the data model, creating a relationship between a root and a child entity in order to achieve my goal. Something that worth considering in some cases.

entry.title: Re: how to export ldm from sap power designer to excel or...
entry.content:
Expanding on Ondrej's answer here - why should you use a list report instead of exporting from a list of objects?

There are several reasons for using a List Report instead of a list of Objects (on the Model menu):

  1. a List report can include information from related objects - a list of model Objects cannot do this. A List report of Entity Attributes can include the description of the Entity that owns an Attribute, and properties from the Domain an Attribute references.
  2. A List report can be created for additional types of Objects, such as Mappings
  3. A List Report is an Object in the Model; it can be copied to another Model.
  4. You can save read-only snapshots (known as 'Result Sets') of the output from a List Report, allowing you to see how things have changed over time (especially if you add a meaningful comment to the snapshot).

entry.title: Re: how to export ldm from sap power designer to excel or...
entry.content: and you can automate the generation of a List Report with a model extension, automatically creating a result set when you do so

entry.title: Avoid hard coding time periods in SAC Planning data actions
entry.content:
We have the following data action to make a forecast for the next remaining months based on the index of the previous months. The index is calculated on past months. This index is used for the calculation of the future months. The below script will calculate the specific "PERIOD_FORECAST" (for example period 10 or 11 or 12) by multiplying the Budget for period 10 times the calculated index over the past 9 periods:

 

// Context
MEMBERSET [d/Measures] = "AMOUNT"
MEMBERSET [d/BEDRIJFSNR] = %COMPANY_FILTER%
MEMBERSET [d/Date] = %PERIOD_FORECAST%
MEMBERSET [d/NIV4] = %RUBRIEK%
 
// Empty data
DATA([d/Date] = %PERIOD_FORECAST%) = 0
 
// Formules
DATA([d/Date] = %PERIOD_FORECAST%) = 
((RESULTLOOKUP([d/Version] = %BASELINE_INDEX_VERSIE%, [d/Date] = "202301") + RESULTLOOKUP([d/Version] = %BASELINE_INDEX_VERSIE%, [d/Date] = "202302") 
+ RESULTLOOKUP([d/Version] = %BASELINE_INDEX_VERSIE%, [d/Date] = "202303") + RESULTLOOKUP([d/Version] = %BASELINE_INDEX_VERSIE%, [d/Date] = "202304") 
+ RESULTLOOKUP([d/Version] = %BASELINE_INDEX_VERSIE%, [d/Date] = "202305") + RESULTLOOKUP([d/Version] = %BASELINE_INDEX_VERSIE%, [d/Date] = "202306")
+ RESULTLOOKUP([d/Version] = %BASELINE_INDEX_VERSIE%, [d/Date] = "202307") + RESULTLOOKUP([d/Version] = %BASELINE_INDEX_VERSIE%, [d/Date] = "202308")
+ RESULTLOOKUP([d/Version] = %BASELINE_INDEX_VERSIE%, [d/Date] = "202309"))
/
(RESULTLOOKUP([d/Version] = %BASELINE_INDEX_VERSIE_VERGELIJK%, [d/Date] = "202301") + RESULTLOOKUP([d/Version] = %BASELINE_INDEX_VERSIE_VERGELIJK%, [d/Date] = "202302") 
+ RESULTLOOKUP([d/Version] = %BASELINE_INDEX_VERSIE_VERGELIJK%, [d/Date] = "202303") + RESULTLOOKUP([d/Version] = %BASELINE_INDEX_VERSIE_VERGELIJK%, [d/Date] = "202304") 
+ RESULTLOOKUP([d/Version] = %BASELINE_INDEX_VERSIE_VERGELIJK%, [d/Date] = "202305") + RESULTLOOKUP([d/Version] = %BASELINE_INDEX_VERSIE_VERGELIJK%, [d/Date] = "202306")
+ RESULTLOOKUP([d/Version] = %BASELINE_INDEX_VERSIE_VERGELIJK%, [d/Date] = "202307") + RESULTLOOKUP([d/Version] = %BASELINE_INDEX_VERSIE_VERGELIJK%, [d/Date] = "202308")
+ RESULTLOOKUP([d/Version] = %BASELINE_INDEX_VERSIE_VERGELIJK%, [d/Date] = "202309")))
* RESULTLOOKUP([d/Version] = %BASELINE_INDEX_VERSIE_VERGELIJK%, [d/Date] = %PERIOD_FORECAST%)

We would like to avoid the hard coded "2023xx" and replace it with a flexible solution

entry.title: Get data with more than 128 chars
entry.content:
Dear all

Using function "XPG_COMMAND_EXECUTE" returns a table with the field "MESSAGE" 128 chars long.

I'm using it to execute the ms-dos command DIR to find some files in a Windows server.

The problem is that the path of some fields is longer than 128.

Is there a function that return a table with more than 128 chars (I need about 240 chars)?

I'm using it like this:

  CALL FUNCTION 'SXPG_COMMAND_EXECUTE'
    EXPORTING
      commandname  = lv_command_name   "-->>  added in SM69  as  ZDIR_CMD    cmd.exe /c dir /S /L /W /B

      additional_parameters = lv_campo   
    TABLES
      exec_protocol         = lt_output
    EXCEPTIONS
      no_permission         = 1

thank you

Daniel

entry.title: Re: SAP CPI JDBC Connection to Azure Synapse DB failure - "The driver received an unexpected pr
entry.content: As stated in the message the driver you are using is only for MS SQL... being a MS product does not mean it's synonymous with what this driver supports.
